finding_results.notes: Cut the triple-restatement loop (lines 467-479 in the pre-revision chapter) where Anya re-paraphrased the contamination thesis and Damian echoed it back twice ('So you don't know... If any of it was yours' / 'And if you can't tell, I definitely can't'). Replaced with Anya's brief 'I don't know,' a thought fragment capturing Damian's fear (*In that room by myself.*), and a physical beat (glass). Damian's single allowed thesis paraphrase remains at line 465 ('You think you might have kissed me because I wanted it badly enough for both of us'). His next substantial line is now 'Have you had that with anyone else' (line 473), which introduces new pressure about her isolation history rather than circling the same ambiguity. Also cut the second thesis restatement at the 'clean answer' section (pre-revision lines 509-519) where Damian returned to the contamination binary; the emotional content of his fear (being alone in the safe house) is now carried by the thought fragment at line 469. The scene advances from the loneliness exchange directly to Damian's forward-looking offering ('I can tell you one thing,' line 501).

summary: Collapsed the composed-diagnosis loop in the second-hour safe-house exchange from four thesis restatements to one. Damian's single paraphrase at line 465 is the only explicit restatement of the contamination ambiguity; after that, the scene advances through a thought fragment, a new question about Anya's isolation, and Damian's concrete offering about the post-gala period. The 'clean answer' thesis restatement was cut entirely, with the emotional core (Damian's fear of having been alone in the experience) relocated to a surface-thought fragment. Net reduction of approximately 20 lines. All payoffs, setups, must-land beats, and chapter-ending state preserved.
